summary: American-born British sculptor, known for Rock Drill, the tomb of Oscar Wilde, Jacob and the Angel, and eighteen nude sculptures for the BMA building on the Strand. Father-in-law of Lucian Freud. Associated with Vorticism.

works.name: Rock Drill (1913–1915)
works.description: Vorticist sculpture of an angular figure mounted on an industrial rock drill. Later reduced to just the torso cast in metal. May have inspired the battle droids in Star Wars prequels. Wikipedia

images.caption: Torso in Metal from The Rock Drill

clues.clue: Vorticist sculpture of an angular figure built upon an industrial rock drill; the artist later removed the arms and bottom to expose just the torso, which was cast in metal
examples: A year after completing one sculpture, this artist removed its arms and most of the bottom part to more fully expose a mechanical device it was built upon
An angular figure in the form of a readymade by this artist was separated from the industrial machinery on which it had once sat and exhibited as a solo torso
Following World War I, this artist cast just the torso of that Vorticist sculpture in metal

clues.clue: May have inspired General Grievous / battle droids in Star Wars prequels
examples: General Grievous resembles a figure that this sculptor placed atop an industrial tool
that work by this artist may have inspired the battle droids in the Star Wars prequels

clues.clue: Torso in Metal shares its name with a section of Ezra Pound's The Cantos
examples: Torso in Metal is part of a sculpture from this country that shares its name with a section of Ezra Pound's The Cantos

works.name: Tomb of Oscar Wilde (1912)
works.description: Funerary monument in Pere Lachaise Cemetery featuring a flying, demonic Assyrian-inspired winged sphinx. Originally featured notably large testicles, which were covered by a plaque. Wikipedia

images.caption: Tomb of Oscar Wilde, Pere Lachaise

clues.clue: Winged sphinx / flying demonic angel for Oscar Wilde's tomb, Assyrian-inspired
examples: This artist sculpted a winged sphinx for the tomb of Oscar Wilde
A flying, demonic angel that once had some notably large testicles zigzags across the facade of this sculptor's tomb for Oscar Wilde
A screen was installed to prevent people kissing a funerary monument, created by this artist, of an Assyrian inspired flying sphinx

clues.clue: Father-in-law of Lucian Freud refused to attend unveiling after Robert Ross placed a plaque over the sculpture's very large testicles
examples: This father-in-law of Lucian Freud refused to attend the unveiling of one of his works after Robert Ross had a plaque placed over a set of very large testicles

works.name: BMA Building Sculptures (1908)
works.description: Eighteen realistically rendered nude sculptures for niches on the British Medical Association building on the Strand. Sparked protests; later damaged when Southern Rhodesia took over the building. Wikipedia

clues.clue: Eighteen nude sculptures for the BMA building on the Strand; sparked protests and were later damaged
examples: This artist sparked protests with eighteen nudes he sculpted for niches on the BMA building on the Strand
After the government of Southern Rhodesia took over the building of the British Medical Association, many of this sculptor's 18 realistically rendered nude men for the building's niches were damaged

clues.clue: Commissioned by architect Charles Holden; Day and Night sculptures also for Holden
examples: those works were commissioned by architect Charles Holden, who had earlier commissioned this man to create eighteen sculptures for the headquarters of the British Medical Association on the Strand

works.name: Jacob and the Angel
works.description: Monumental alabaster sculpture of the Biblical Jacob wrestling an angel. Purchased by Tussaud's Waxworks rather than the Tate, to the artist's dismay.

clues.clue: Alabaster sculpture of Jacob wrestling an angel; acquired by Tussaud's Waxworks instead of the Tate
examples: This artist complained when his alabaster depiction of Jacob and the Angel was acquired by Tussaud's Waxworks rather than the Tate
Another monumental sculpture created from the same block of alabaster by this man is housed in the Tate and depicts a fatigued biblical namesake supported by an angel

works.name: General / Biographical
works.description: American-born British sculptor. Father-in-law of Lucian Freud. Testified on behalf of Brancusi's Bird in Space. Other works include St. Michael and the Devil (Coventry Cathedral), Genesis, The Rush of Green, Ecce Homo, Rima the Bird Girl memorial.

examples: This father-in-law of Lucian Freud refused to attend the unveiling

clues.clue: Testified on behalf of Brancusi's Bird in Space at customs trial, saying 'If it is beautiful it could not have been made by a mechanic'
examples: This artist came to testify on behalf of Brancusi's Bird in Space, stating that 'If it is beautiful it could not have been made by a mechanic.'

clues.clue: St. Michael and the Devil sculpture at Coventry Cathedral; also Ecce Homo there
examples: The east wall of this church features a sculpture of St. Michael's victory over the Devil
He depicts a winged-man standing over a figure with horns in St. Michael and the Devil, which is located at the Coventry Cathedral

clues.clue: The Risen Christ: adapted from portrait of friend Bernard van Dieren into anti-war depiction
examples: This artist adapted a portrait of his friend Bernard van Dieren into an anti-war depiction of The Risen Christ that was free from religious symbols

clues.clue: Arthur Conan Doyle launched petition to remove his Rima the Bird Girl memorial
examples: Arthur Conan Doyle launched a petition to remove a memorial that this artist created depicting a bare-chested version of Rima the Bird Girl

comprehensive_summary:
Jacob Epstein was an American-born British sculptor active in the early-to-mid 20th century, associated with Vorticism and modernist sculpture. He is most commonly identified as the sculptor of Rock Drill and the tomb of Oscar Wilde.

Rock Drill is his most frequently clued work, a Vorticist sculpture that originally depicted an angular, mechanical figure mounted on an actual industrial rock drill. After World War I, Epstein removed the arms and lower portion, casting just the torso in metal. The sculpture's robotic appearance has been compared to General Grievous and the battle droids from Star Wars. The work also shares its name (Torso in Metal) with a section of Ezra Pound's The Cantos.

His tomb for Oscar Wilde at Pere Lachaise Cemetery features an Assyrian-inspired flying winged sphinx. The monument originally had notably large testicles, which Robert Ross covered with a plaque, causing Epstein to refuse to attend the unveiling. A screen was later installed to prevent people from kissing the monument.

Epstein created eighteen realistically rendered nude sculptures for the BMA building on the Strand, commissioned by architect Charles Holden. These sparked immediate protests, and many were damaged when the government of Southern Rhodesia took over the building. His monumental alabaster Jacob and the Angel was acquired by Tussaud's Waxworks rather than the Tate, to his dismay.

Other clued works include St. Michael and the Devil and Ecce Homo at Coventry Cathedral; Genesis (a nude pregnant woman in alabaster); The Rush of Green in Hyde Park; The Risen Christ (adapted from a portrait of his friend Bernard van Dieren); Day and Night; and the Rima the Bird Girl memorial, which Arthur Conan Doyle petitioned to remove.

Epstein was the father-in-law of Lucian Freud. He testified on behalf of Brancusi's Bird in Space during the famous customs trial. He sculpted a bronze portrait bust of Paul Robeson, during which Robeson sang lullabies to Epstein's daughter Peggy Jean. He wrote the autobiography Let There Be Sculpture.
